summary: Decompiled a large part of src/melee/pl/plbonus.c, primarily replacing the fn_80039618 placeholder with a substantial C implementation of player bonus/stat flag calculation. The PR also refined pl_804D6470_t in src/melee/pl/types.h so plbonus thresholds/config values could be accessed with typed offset fields, added a dont_inline pragma around fn_80038700, and adjusted arithmetic in fn_8003CC84 to improve matching. The bot report showed plbonus .data and .sdata2 reaching 100%, fn_8003CC84 reaching 100%, and fn_80039618 improving from 0.00% to 99.67%. No human review feedback was present in the slice.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; added fn_80039618 implementation, tuned fn_80038700 inlining, and rewrote digit extraction in fn_8003CC84.
Expanded pl_804D6470_t from byte padding into typed fields used by plbonus threshold comparisons.

what_changed: Replaced the placeholder comment for fn_80039618 with a large C implementation that computes many player bonus flags via setFlag(player, id).
fn_80039618 now reads pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t, plActionStats, HSD_GObj, hit counts, attack counts, stale-move table values, KO/fall data, and game-mode helpers to determine bonus conditions.
Added #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around fn_80038700, preserving it as a standalone helper instead of allowing inlining.
Changed fn_8003CC84 digit decomposition from chained modulo expressions to subtractive arithmetic: hundreds, then tens from temp_ret - hundreds*100, then ones from the remaining value.
Expanded pl_804D6470_t fields at offsets x0, x8, xC, x10, x20, x50, x54, xF0, and x160 from opaque padding to float/u32 fields used by the new plbonus logic.
The bot match report recorded plbonus .data +400 bytes to 100%, plbonus .sdata2 +24 bytes to 100%, fn_8003CC84 +34 bytes to 100%, and fn_80039618 +6678 bytes to 99.67%.
smart_moves: Progressively typed only the needed pl_804D6470_t offsets instead of over-naming unknown config fields, enabling correct codegen while keeping uncertain semantics explicit.

Cached frequently used plActionStats subfields into locals at the top of fn_80039618, including attacks, hits, x358_hits, totals, and count arrays, which likely helped reproduce original register allocation and readability.

Used explicit loop bounds and numeric action/bonus IDs from the binary, such as action ranges 1..0x10, 0x33..0x3D, 0x40..0x62, and bonus flags like 0x94, 0x95, 0x96, rather than inventing unsupported names.
Kept matching-oriented control flow with labels such as skip_only_low_moves and skip_combo_count where early exits/gotos better matched the original structure.
Applied #pragma dont_inline specifically to fn_80038700, showing that preserving call boundaries can be necessary even for small summation helpers.
Used PAD_STACK(16) at the end of fn_80039618, indicating a deliberate stack-layout/matching adjustment rather than purely source-level cleanup.
Rewrote division/modulo digit extraction in fn_8003CC84 into equivalent subtractive expressions, a small but effective codegen-sensitive match tactic.
decomp_lessons: Large bonus-calculation functions can be made nearly matching by first exposing typed threshold/config offsets in the relevant global config struct, even if field semantics remain unknown.
When a helper is small and attractive to the compiler, local pragmas like #pragma dont_inline can be an appropriate narrow fix to preserve expected calls without affecting the whole file.
Modulo-heavy arithmetic may need to be rewritten into subtraction from previously computed quotient products to match compiler output.
For control-flow-heavy scoring logic, retaining goto labels for skip paths can be more faithful than forcing structured C.
Use existing project conventions for uncertain symbols: keep fn_-address names for unknown functions and x-offset field names for unknown struct fields until semantics are proven.
Explicit casts between f32, s32, and u32 matter in threshold comparisons and average calculations; the new code uses casts around max/min, damage, KOs, and stale-table values.
A near-match can still deliver useful global progress: the bot reported fn_80039618 at 99.67% and associated data sections at 100%, even though the function itself was not fully complete.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions remain address-based, e.g. fn_80039618, fn_80038700, fn_8003CC84.
Known project API names use subsystem prefixes and Pascal-style verbs, e.g. Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2, Player_GetActionStats, Player_GetEntity, Player_GetFalls, Player_GetKOsByPlayerIndex.
Unknown struct members keep offset names such as x0_staleMoveTable, x904, xC60, xC64, xC74, xC78, xC94, xD04, xD0C, xDC8, xDD1, and pl_804D6470_t fields like x20, x50, xF0, x160.

Locals in the decompiled function are descriptive where semantics are clear enough, e.g. aerials, atk_thrown, hit_thrown, attacks_total, hits_total, x358_total, found_low, found_high, max_ratio, sum_ratio.
Uncertain counters tied to known offsets retain offset-bearing names, e.g. hit_x1A8, atk_x1A8, hit_x1A0, x358_x1A8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline on was scoped around fn_80038700 to prevent an unwanted inline transformation.
PAD_STACK(16) was added in fn_80039618 to satisfy stack layout/codegen requirements.
Goto labels were used for binary-like early exits: skip_only_low_moves and skip_combo_count.
Digit splitting in fn_8003CC84 was rewritten from temp_ret % 100 expressions to subtraction using temp_r5 * 100 and temp_r3_3 * 10.
Typed pl_804D6470_t offsets replaced opaque byte arrays so comparisons could use the correct load types, including float and u32.
The implementation uses exact loop ranges and constants from the target logic, including 99-count loops, action indices 1..0x10, and many numeric setFlag IDs.
Explicit casts to f32, s32, and u32 are used in comparisons to influence emitted instructions and signedness/float conversion behavior.

review_feedback.feedback: Match report for GALE01 showed matched code 67.85% (+0.08%, +2980 bytes) and matched data 39.68% (+0.04%, +456 bytes).
Reported 6 new matches, including main/melee/pl/plbonus .data to 100%, main/melee/pl/plbonus .sdata2 to 100%, and main/melee/pl/plbonus fn_8003CC84 to 100%.
Reported fn_80039618 improved from 0.00% to 99.67% with +6678 bytes.
No human review comments or review approvals were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish fn_80039618 from 99.67% to 100%.
Find PRs that rename or further type pl_804D6470_t fields used by plbonus.
Search for other uses of #pragma dont_inline in melee decomp matching fixes.
Search for PAD_STACK usage in large player/stat functions.
Investigate whether bonus flag IDs in fn_80039618 were later given enum names.
